provenance.notes: Identifier terminology reference. This file documents known identifier spaces and conversion status; bytes5 rescue-order conversion is array-backed by reviewed library/dataset/API/contract sources, not derived by formula. Current Acclimated ERC-721 token ID behavior is scoped to that contract only; older wrapper token ID conventions remain unverified.

evidence: Local source sub100 is exactly 0 through 99.
Local source day1 and week1 buckets are contiguous from 0.
Imported rescue bucket arrays in data/rescue-buckets.json match mooncat_rescueOrder_by_category.json exactly.
Imported character-cat arrays in data/character-cat-index.json match mooncat_rescueOrder_by_category.json exactly for checked categories.
Preferred API samples for 0, 82, 3364, 3365, 5683, 5684, 5754, 5755, and 25439 returned rescueOrder equal to the requested numeric index.
Preferred API year-boundary samples returned rescueYear values matching local year bucket boundaries.
Preferred API genesis samples at 84 and 2891 returned genesis: true with rescueOrder equal to the requested index.
limitations: This verifies numeric alignment for sampled API records and local bucket structure; it is not a full exhaustive API crawl.
This does not define conversion to bytes5 cat IDs, ERC-721 token IDs, OpenSea IDs, wrapper IDs, or contract call values.

evidence: Etherscan page for 0x60cd862c9C687A9dE49aecdC3A99b74A4fc54aB6 identifies a verified contract address with public name tag MoonCatRescue: MoonCatRescue.
Etherscan page for 0xc3f733ca98E0daD0386979Eb96fb1722A1A05E69 identifies Acclimated Mooncats as a verified token contract.
LooksRare collection URL uses 0xc3f733ca98E0daD0386979Eb96fb1722A1A05E69 for MoonCats.
OpenSea item URLs sampled for token IDs 0, 82, and 25439 use the acclimated contract address and the numeric token ID in the URL.
Acclimated MoonCatAcclimator source says wrap(_rescueOrder) returns the ID/rescue order of the minted token.
Acclimated MoonCatAcclimator source mints _tokenId in _wrap(address _owner, uint256 _tokenId), where _tokenId is documented as the rescue order of the MoonCat.

Preferred API /mooncat/erc721-metadata/{index} samples for 0, 82, and 25439 returned ERC721-style metadata whose name, external_url, image URL, and Rescue Index attribute use the requested numeric rescue index.
limitations: OpenSea token ID behavior is verified for sampled acclimated MoonCat item URLs, not for unrelated collections or historical wrappers.
This does not define conversion from rescue order/token ID to bytes5 catId.
This does not verify the older wrapper contract token ID convention beyond the Acclimated source context that an older wrapper existed.

evidence: Preferred API OpenAPI defines MoonCatIdentifier as either a five-byte hex string or decimal index value for /mooncat/traits/{catId}.
Preferred API samples for numeric inputs 0, 82, 84, and 25439 returned bytes5 catId values and matching rescueOrder values.
Preferred API samples for bytes5 inputs 0x00d658d50b, 0x0057774705, and 0x0076fe2589 returned the same catId and rescueOrder values 0, 82, and 25439.
Preferred API numeric sample 84 returned catId 0xff00000ca7 with genesis: true and rescueOrder 84.
Original MoonCatRescue source declares public bytes5[25600] rescueOrder and writes cat IDs into rescueOrder[rescueIndex] when cats are rescued or genesis cats are added.
MoonCatTraits catIdOf(uint256 rescueOrder) requires rescueOrder < 25440 and returns MCR.rescueOrder(rescueOrder).
MoonCatSVGs rescueOrder overloads require rescueOrder < 25440 and call MoonCatRescue.rescueOrder(rescueOrder) before using the bytes5 catId.
references/upstream/mooncatrescue/mooncat_traits.json validates as JSON with 25,440 rows; checked rows are ordered by rescueOrder and catId values are unique.
mooncat_traits.json samples match existing API samples for rescueOrder 0, 82, 84, and 25439.
references/upstream/mooncatrescue/libmooncat-limited.js exports getMoonCatIdByRescueIndex, getCatId, getRescueOrder, parseCatId, and typed getTraits helpers.
LibMoonCat getMoonCatIdByRescueIndex/getCatId samples map 0, 82, 84, and 25439 to 0x00d658d50b, 0x0057774705, 0xff00000ca7, and 0x0076fe2589.
LibMoonCat getRescueOrder samples map 0x00d658d50b, 0x0057774705, 0xff00000ca7, and 0x0076fe2589 back to 0, 82, 84, and 25439.

lookupMethods.method: Use an array-backed lookup: read mooncat_traits.json[rescueOrder].catId, call LibMoonCat.getMoonCatIdByRescueIndex(rescueOrder) / getCatId(rescueOrder), call preferred API /mooncat/traits/{catId_or_rescueIndex} with a decimal rescue index and read catId, or call MoonCatTraits.catIdOf(rescueOrder) / original MoonCatRescue.rescueOrder(rescueOrder).

Use an array-backed reverse lookup over the reference dataset, call LibMoonCat.getRescueOrder(0x-prefixed catId), or call preferred API /mooncat/traits/{catId_or_rescueIndex} with a bytes5 catId and read rescueOrder.

limitations: This verifies array-backed library/dataset conversion and sampled values, not a closed-form reverse formula from bytes5 catId to rescue order.
This does not import a full 25,440-entry mapping table.
The on-chain sources reviewed here expose rescueOrder-to-catId lookup; they do not establish an on-chain reverse lookup from bytes5 catId to rescue order.
The local upstream reference files are registered as reference copies, not curated KB data.
Checked LibMoonCat getRescueOrder calls resolved 0x-prefixed cat IDs; non-prefixed cat IDs should be normalized with parseCatId before relying on lookup behavior.
Token-facing conversion remains scoped to the current Acclimated MoonCats contract, where tokenId is already separately verified as rescue order.

identifierKinds.label: bytes5 MoonCat ID
identifierKinds.notes: The contract source uses bytes5 cat IDs. The API and OpenAPI docs document MoonCatIdentifier inputs as either bytes5 cat IDs or decimal rescue indexes. Preferred API samples, LibMoonCat helpers, and the mooncat_traits.json reference dataset verify array-backed bytes5 catId to rescueOrder lookup; the current Acclimated token ID convention then maps that rescue order to tokenId for the current wrapper only. This file does not define a closed-form reverse formula from bytes5 catId to rescue order.

identifierKinds.label: API original rescue index
identifierKinds.notes: The API landing pages document rescueIndex values from 0 through 25439 for catId_or_rescueIndex. Local bucket checks and preferred API samples support alignment between this API rescueOrder convention and local rescue-order-index values. Preferred API samples, contract source paths, LibMoonCat helpers, and mooncat_traits.json verify lookup from this numeric convention to bytes5 catId, but not a standalone closed-form derivation.

identifierKinds.label: local rescue-order index
identifierKinds.notes: Local membership and rescue bucket files use indexKind rescue-order-index. Local bucket checks and preferred API samples support alignment with the API rescueOrder/original rescue index convention. Use the verified array-backed lookup methods before using these values as bytes5 catIds. They are token IDs only for the current Acclimated MoonCats contract, not generic OpenSea IDs or generic contract-call values.

identifierKinds.label: Acclimated ERC-721 token ID
usedBy: marketplace item URLs for the acclimated collection
identifierKinds.notes: For the current Acclimated MoonCats contract, Etherscan source shows the ERC-721 tokenId is the MoonCat rescue order. Because rescue order to bytes5 catId lookup is now verified through array-backed library/dataset, API, and contract sources, current Acclimated token IDs can be looked up to bytes5 catIds through that rescue-order convention. This does not apply automatically to older wrappers or unrelated token-facing surfaces.

identifierKinds.label: older wrapper token ID
usedBy: older MoonCat wrapper surfaces when specifically identified
evidenceNeeded: verified source or ABI for that wrapper
mint/wrap/unwrap token ID code path or contract documentation
sampled marketplace/API evidence tied to that exact contract if marketplace behavior is being claimed
warnings: Do not reuse the current Acclimated MoonCats tokenId equals rescue order rule for older wrappers.
Do not assume older wrapper token IDs match rescue order, bytes5 catId, OpenSea URL IDs, or local rescue-order indexes.
Do not convert older wrapper token IDs without first identifying the exact contract and source-backed convention.
identifierKinds.notes: The allowed existing sources for this pass do not verify an older wrapper token ID convention. This kind is included to prevent accidental generalization from the current Acclimated contract.

identifierKinds.label: OpenSea token ID
usedBy: OpenSea item URLs for the acclimated MoonCats collection
identifierKinds.notes: Sampled OpenSea item URLs use /item/ethereum/0xc3f733ca98e0dad0386979eb96fb1722a1a05e69/{tokenId}; token IDs 0, 82, and 25439 resolved to MoonCat pages with matching numeric names/metadata. Treat this as sampled evidence for the acclimated collection, not a blanket marketplace rule.

identifierKinds.label: Original MoonCatRescue contract address
identifierKinds.notes: Verified as the Ethereum mainnet original MoonCatRescue contract address from the registered Etherscan source. This is a contract address, not a MoonCat identifier.

identifierKinds.label: Acclimated MoonCats contract address
identifierKinds.notes: Verified from the registered Etherscan source and marketplace URLs. This is a contract address, not a token ID.

identifierKinds.label: Ethereum address
identifierKinds.notes: Used for owner/profile and owned-MoonCat API lookups. This file does not document checksum, lowercase, or ENS normalization behavior.

identifierKinds.label: accessory ID
identifierKinds.notes: API paths document accessoryId parameters. This pass does not define accessory ID format, range, contract origin, or validation rules.

knownParameters.notes: API landing pages document this parameter as accepting either a valid MoonCat ID or original rescue index.

warnings: Local rescue-order indexes are numerically aligned with API rescueOrder/original rescue index for checked data, but agents should still name the API form when constructing API requests.
Do not pass token-facing IDs to catId_or_rescueIndex parameters unless the token ID has first been verified as the same rescue-order convention for the exact surface.

acceptedBy: sampled marketplace item URLs for the acclimated collection
warnings: This is the only token-facing wrapper convention currently verified in this file.
Do not generalize this convention to older wrappers, unrelated ERC-721 contracts, or marketplace pages for other collections.
No older wrapper token ID mapping is verified from the allowed existing sources.
An older wrapper contract address alone would not be enough; the token ID mint/wrap convention must be reviewed.
limitations: The sourceRef only supports the boundary warning that Acclimated evidence should not be generalized; it does not verify older wrapper behavior.

acceptedBy: sampled OpenSea item URLs for the acclimated MoonCats collection
crossSurfaceBoundaries.exampleShape: collection contract address plus decimal URL token segment
warnings: Marketplace URL token segments are scoped to the collection contract in the URL.
Do not infer broader OpenSea, LooksRare, Blur, or older-wrapper token behavior from sampled Acclimated OpenSea URLs.
Local array indexes are not bytes5 cat IDs.
Local array indexes are not generic token IDs.
Apply the target-surface conversion rule before using local values outside curated local data files.

conversionRules.notes: Local bucket structure and preferred API samples support numeric alignment between API rescueOrder/original rescue index and local rescue-order-index. This does not imply conversion to bytes5 catIds or token-facing IDs.

Local rescue-order-index values can be treated as API rescueOrder/original rescue index values for the checked convention. This does not make them token IDs, OpenSea IDs, bytes5 catIds, or contract call values.

Local rescue-order indexes are aligned with API rescueOrder/original rescue index values for the checked convention. A bytes5 catId can be looked up by array index in the registered mooncat_traits.json reference dataset, LibMoonCat getMoonCatIdByRescueIndex/getCatId, the preferred API, or the original MoonCatRescue rescueOrder(uint256) public array / MoonCatTraits catIdOf(uint256). This is array-backed lookup, not a closed-form derivation.

mooncat_traits.json[rescueOrder].catId and LibMoonCat getMoonCatIdByRescueIndex/getCatId provide array-backed lookup; preferred API samples for numeric inputs returned catId fields, and MoonCatTraits.catIdOf(uint256) returns MCR.rescueOrder(rescueOrder). Numeric inputs must be valid rescue-order values under 25440.

The registered mooncat_traits.json reference dataset can be reverse-indexed by catId, LibMoonCat getRescueOrder resolves checked 0x-prefixed cat IDs, and preferred API samples for bytes5 catId inputs returned matching rescueOrder values. No on-chain reverse lookup or closed-form formula is documented here.

For the current Acclimated MoonCats contract only, use array-backed dataset/library lookup or the preferred API to look up rescueOrder from bytes5 catId; that rescueOrder is the current Acclimated ERC-721 token ID by the separately verified token convention.

For the current Acclimated MoonCats contract only, tokenId is rescue order; use array-backed dataset/library lookup, API lookup, or contract rescueOrder lookup to get the bytes5 catId. This does not apply to older wrappers.
For the current Acclimated MoonCats ERC-721 contract, tokenId is the rescue order/local rescue-order index. This does not apply automatically to older wrappers or unrelated contracts.
Sampled OpenSea acclimated collection URLs use the acclimated contract address plus numeric token ID matching rescue order. This is sampled marketplace evidence, not a general OpenSea rule.

No older wrapper token ID convention is verified from the allowed existing sources. Identify the exact older wrapper contract and review its source/ABI or documented token ID semantics before attempting conversion.
Do not assume rescue order is the older wrapper token ID. The rescue-order token convention is verified only for the current Acclimated MoonCats contract.
The current Acclimated ERC-721 token ID and any older wrapper token ID are separate contract-scoped identifier spaces unless a reviewed source proves an equivalence.
